name: Login / Sign-in Entry Point
description: Discoverable, working sign-in path from the main site for an existing customer to authenticate.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09. frontend/index.html's cdbApplyGates() (the same idempotent function that already injected a 'Dashboard' nav link for authenticated visitors) now has the previously-missing else-branch: a logged-out visitor gets a real 'Sign In' link injected into both #cdb-nav-actions (desktop) and #nav-mobile-drawer (mobile), pointing at frontend/user-dashboard.html — whose #login-overlay shows automatically whenever no valid session token is present (frontend/user-dashboard.html:1997-2003). The link is removed again on the cdb:login event so it never lingers post-login (verified live via Playwright: link present when logged out, absent + Dashboard link present after simulated login). The dead-end 'Sign In Required' modal (createMonitorModal(), formerly frontend/index.html:13154-13157) now has a working 'Sign In' button that navigates to frontend/user-dashboard.html, plus a separate 'Cancel' button preserving the original dismiss behavior. A THIRD dead end, reported live by a prospect hitting a reused scan token (2026-07-09): the 403 error banner's 'log in' link called showModal('loginModal') — an id that never existed anywhere in the file (grep-confirmed, showModal() itself works fine elsewhere) — replaced with a real href to frontend/user-dashboard.html.

navigation.evidence: Sign In is now a real, visible, correctly-labeled nav entry on both desktop (#cdb-nav-actions) and mobile (#nav-mobile-drawer) for any logged-out visitor — dynamically injected by cdbApplyGates(), same mechanism/reliability as the pre-existing authenticated-user Dashboard link.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/homepageSignInPath.test.mjs (2026-07-09, new) — 3 tests reading frontend/index.html directly (same convention as workers/test/autoSocToggleGuard.test.mjs): the Sign In link is injected for logged-out visitors on both desktop and mobile, it is removed again once authenticated, and the dead-end modal's button now navigates to the login page while the dismiss/Cancel path still exists. Plus the pre-existing workers/test/gdprAccountDeletion.test.mjs, workers/test/emailWiringTask3Phase1.test.mjs, and workers/test/phase10PasswordReset.test.mjs (backend handlers, unchanged).

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09 (own commit, own regression tests). frontend/index.html read directly; both the cdbApplyGates() fix and the createMonitorModal() fix independently re-verified with a real headless-Chromium Playwright session (not just static grep) run against the changed file served locally: confirmed the Sign In link renders with correct text/href on both desktop and mobile, confirmed it disappears and the Dashboard link appears after a simulated cdb:login event (no stale UI, no regression to the existing authenticated-user path), confirmed the modal renders a working 'Sign In' button plus 'Cancel'; zero uncaught JS errors during the whole session. A follow-up same-day commit fixed the third dead end (scan-error 'log in' link) with an identical live-Playwright verification (isolated the correct anchor among the now-multiple same-target links on the page, confirmed onclick attribute removed). Full backend suite green: 180 files / 1902 tests, extended to 4 with the third fix. scripts/seo-structure-lock.mjs: all 22 pages green (unaffected).

contradicts_doc.claim: Auth: signup / login / session / logout / change-password | GA APPROVED | Live-verified incl. negative paths across Phases IX–X
contradicts_doc.correction: "Live-verified" here means the API endpoints were exercised directly (dynamic_api), not that a customer could find and use the login form from the production site — the dead-end-modal / buried-footer-link finding this correction described is now fixed (2026-07-09). customer_journey_complete stays false pending a real dynamic_browser verification pass against production, not because the fix itself is in doubt.

notes: Classified P1 rather than P2 because this is the single entry point every other authenticated capability depends on — a customer who cannot find sign-in cannot reach anything else, regardless of how complete the rest of the platform is. Fixed as a minimally-disruptive, additive change: the existing authenticated-user Dashboard-link mechanism and the in-page #dashboard section (both already working) were left completely untouched — only the missing logged-out case was added, following the codebase's own established idempotent-injection pattern rather than introducing a new one.

name: Sign-Up / Account Creation Entry Point
description: Discoverable, working sign-up path from the sign-in screen for a prospective customer with no existing credentials to create an account.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09. Reported live as a Microsoft customer escalation: a prospective customer with no existing credentials clicking 'No account? Get started free' on the login overlay (frontend/user-dashboard.html) was sent to href="/" — the homepage — with no signup form anywhere on the site. Confirmed by exhaustive grep before the fix: zero matches anywhere in frontend/ for 'api/auth/signup', 'doSignup', 'signup-view', or any signup modal id, despite POST /api/auth/signup (handleSignup) being a complete, already-production-grade implementation (password hashing, duplicate-email check, rollback-on-partial-failure, welcome email, auto-provisioned first API key) wired at workers/src/index.js since before this fix. Root cause: the backend capability was built; no frontend surface ever called it. Fixed by adding a #signup-view to the existing #login-overlay (name/email/company/password, following the exact CSS/markup conventions of the pre-existing login/forgot/reset views) and a doSignup() function mirroring doLogin()'s exact fetch/error/spinner pattern, wired to the existing, unmodified backend endpoint. 'Get started free' now opens this view via showAuthView('signup-view') instead of navigating away.

navigation.evidence: Reachable from the login overlay's 'No account? Get started free' link (frontend/user-dashboard.html), the only pre-existing surface referencing account creation on the site. Symmetric 'Already have an account? Sign in' link added to return to login-view.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/userDashboardSignupAndMfa.test.mjs (2026-07-09, new) — 7 signup-specific tests reading frontend/user-dashboard.html, workers/src/index.js, and workers/src/handlers/auth.js directly: the dead href is gone, 'Get started free' opens signup-view, the view has the real fields, showAuthView() recognizes it, doSignup() posts the fields handleSignup actually reads, success stores tokens and calls initDashboard() exactly like doLogin(), and the backend route/handler genuinely exist and read those fields. Independently re-verified live via a real headless-Chromium Playwright session: full happy-path signup (fill form → POST verified with correct body → tokens stored → overlay hidden) and the duplicate-email error path (backend error message surfaced, overlay stays up, no false-positive login).

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09 (own commit, own regression tests, same session as the CAP-IDN-003 MFA fix). Static: frontend/user-dashboard.html, workers/src/index.js, workers/src/handlers/auth.js read directly. Dynamic: real headless-Chromium Playwright session against the changed file served locally — signup happy path (name/email/company/password → POST /api/auth/signup with correct JSON body → 201 → tokens saved to sessionStorage → overlay hidden) and signup failure path (409 duplicate email → backend message shown in #signup-err → overlay stays up) both exercised and passed; zero uncaught JS exceptions. axe-core WCAG2A/AA scan run against the new view: found and fixed one real new issue (link-in-text-block on the 'Already have an account? Sign in' link — added text-decoration:underline, matching the fix applied symmetrically to the pre-existing 'Get started free' link); all other flagged violations are a pre-existing, site-wide '--muted' color-contrast issue present identically on the untouched login-view/forgot-view baseline (out of scope for this fix — see notes). Full backend suite green: 181 files / 1916 tests (180/1903 baseline + 1 new file/13 tests). scripts/seo-structure-lock.mjs: all 22 pages green (unaffected, frontend-only change outside <head>).

contradicts_doc.claim: Auth: signup / login / session / logout / change-password | GA APPROVED | Live-verified incl. negative paths across Phases IX–X
contradicts_doc.correction: "Live-verified" here means POST /api/auth/signup was exercised directly (dynamic_api) — it was never claiming a customer could reach a signup form from the production site, and none existed anywhere until this fix (2026-07-09). customer_journey_complete stays false pending a real dynamic_browser verification pass against production (not staging/local), not because the fix itself is in doubt.

notes: Classified P1 (the schema's highest tier) — arguably more severe than CAP-IDN-001's entry-point discoverability fix, because this wasn't a broken link to a working feature, it was the complete absence of any way for a net-new customer to become a customer at all, reported as a live Microsoft escalation. Fixed as a minimally-disruptive, additive change: zero modifications to the existing, working POST /api/auth/signup backend or to the login/forgot/reset views; only the missing signup surface was added, reusing the login overlay's exact established markup/CSS/JS conventions rather than introducing a new pattern or a separate page. Known, deliberately out-of-scope follow-up: a site-wide 'color-contrast' issue on the '--muted' design token (#64748b on #111827, 3.72:1 vs the 4.5:1 WCAG AA minimum) affects every .form-label and .login-sub across all five auth views (login/signup/forgot/reset/mfa) — confirmed pre-existing (identical on the untouched login-view baseline) and out of scope for an auth-flow bug fix; would need a deliberate design-token change reviewed against every page using --muted, not a point fix here.

name: MFA Second-Factor Login Completion
description: A customer with 2FA/TOTP enabled can actually complete sign-in after their password is accepted, by submitting their authenticator code.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09. Found while tracing doLogin() to build the CAP-IDN-002 signup view. handleLogin() (workers/src/handlers/auth.js) returns HTTP 200 with { mfa_required: true, mfa_challenge_token } — no access_token — whenever the account has MFA enabled (checked via issueMFAChallenge()). frontend/user-dashboard.html's doLogin() never checked the response for mfa_required; since res.ok was true for the 200, it fell through to the success path with _token set to undefined, hid the login overlay, and called initDashboard(), which then 401'd calling GET /api/auth/me with no refresh token available and silently bounced the customer back to the login screen with no explanation. POST /api/auth/mfa/authenticate (handleMFAAuthenticate) already existed, already fully tested for its auth-gating (workers/test/mfaAuthGate.test.mjs), and returns the identical token shape as handleLogin's success response — nothing in the frontend ever called it. Fixed by adding a #mfa-view (6-digit code field) and doMfaVerify(), reusing doLogin()'s exact success-handling pattern (saveTokens → hide overlay → initDashboard()).

navigation.evidence: Automatic — doLogin() now routes to #mfa-view via showAuthView('mfa-view') the moment the backend responds with mfa_required:true; no customer action needed to discover it, matching how the reset-view auto-shows on a reset_token query param.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/userDashboardSignupAndMfa.test.mjs (2026-07-09, new) — 6 MFA-specific tests: doLogin() checks mfa_required before trusting the response as a successful login (and does so before any token is stored), #mfa-view exists with the code field wired to doMfaVerify(), showAuthView() recognizes mfa-view, doMfaVerify() posts to the real /api/auth/mfa/authenticate with the challenge token + typed code, success stores tokens and initializes the dashboard exactly like doLogin(), and the backend route/handler genuinely return the same token shape as login. Independently re-verified live via Playwright: password-accepted-but-MFA-required response correctly routes to mfa-view (overlay stays up, zero token stored mid-challenge — previously this silently 'succeeded' with an undefined token), submitting the code completes login with the real token, and a plain non-MFA login is provably unaffected by the change. Plus the pre-existing workers/test/mfaAuthGate.test.mjs (unchanged, covers the endpoint's own auth gating).

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09 (same commit/session as CAP-IDN-002). Static: frontend/user-dashboard.html, workers/src/index.js, workers/src/handlers/auth.js, workers/src/handlers/mfa.js read directly. Dynamic: real headless-Chromium Playwright session — mocked POST /api/auth/login returning mfa_required:true, confirmed the UI correctly switches to the code-entry view and stores no token; mocked POST /api/auth/mfa/authenticate returning real tokens, confirmed login completes; separately confirmed a plain non-MFA login response is handled identically to before the change. Zero uncaught JS exceptions. Full backend suite green: 181 files / 1916 tests, no changes to any backend handler (frontend-only fix consuming an already-correct, already-tested endpoint).

notes: Classified P1 (not P0) because it only blocks the subset of customers who have opted into MFA — likely a small population pre-launch — versus CAP-IDN-002 which blocked every net-new customer. Zero backend changes: POST /api/auth/mfa/authenticate was already correct and already tested; this was purely a missing frontend consumer of an existing capability, the same bug class as CAP-IDN-001's three dead links.
